---
title: "Probability"
author: "Antigravity"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
throw_dice <- function(n, weights = rep(1/6, 6)) {
  die1 <- sample(1:6, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = weights)
  die2 <- sample(1:6, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = weights)
  die1 + die2
}
```

```{r}
set.seed(10)
tibble(n = c(10, 20, 50, 100, 1000)) |>
  mutate(sum = map(n, ~ throw_dice(.x, weights = c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.25)))) |>
  unnest(cols = c(sum)) |>
  mutate(n = factor(n, levels = c(10, 20, 50, 100, 1000))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sum)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "grey50") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2:12) +
  facet_wrap(~ n, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  labs(title = "Simulation of two loaded dice rolls as sample size grows",
       x = "Sum of two dice", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
og_lr <- function(rolls) {
  loaded <- c(0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.30)
  log(loaded[rolls] / (1/6))
}
log_lr <- og_lr
```

```{r}
set.seed(10)
rolls_200 <- sample(1:6, 200, replace = TRUE,
                    prob = c(0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.30))

tibble(
  roll      = 1:200,
  log_odds  = cumsum(log_lr(rolls_200)),
  posterior = 1 / (1 + exp(-log_odds))
) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = roll, y = posterior)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.95, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1),
                     labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Posterior probability that the die is loaded, roll by roll",
       subtitle = "Starts at 50%; each roll nudges the line up (a 6) or down (anything else)",
       x = "Roll number",
       y = "Posterior probability of \"loaded\"") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Introduction

This is a probability analysis.
